title: bootstrapping
code: library(EmStanS) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300)    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2… #> # ℹ 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 × 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # ℹ 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 × 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level1 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level2 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2 #> # ℹ 2,245 more rows #>  #> $item_data #> # A tibble: 90 × 7 #>   GCA   Domain Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50 #>   <chr> <chr>  <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl> #> 1 M1    Alg    M1_1               1     1                   1     102. #> 2 M1    Prob   M1_2               1     1                   2     107. #> 3 M1    Calc   M1_3               1     1                   3     110. #> 4 M1    Geom   M1_4               1     1                   4     113. #> 5 M1    Geom   M1_5               1     1                   5     116. #> # ℹ 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 664 × 3 #>   score  freq Grade #>   <int> <int> <chr> #> 1    86     8 M1    #> 2    87    11 M1    #> 3    88     4 M1    #> 4    89     3 M1    #> 5    90     3 M1    #> # ℹ 659 more rows  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                grade = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

title: getting-started

title: getting-started
code: library(EmStanS) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300)    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2… #> # ℹ 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 × 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # ℹ 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 × 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level1 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level2 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2 #> # ℹ 2,245 more rows #>  #> $item_data #> # A tibble: 90 × 7 #>   GCA   Domain Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50 #>   <chr> <chr>  <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl> #> 1 M1    Alg    M1_1               1     1                   1     102. #> 2 M1    Prob   M1_2               1     1                   2     107. #> 3 M1    Calc   M1_3               1     1                   3     110. #> 4 M1    Geom   M1_4               1     1                   4     113. #> 5 M1    Geom   M1_5               1     1                   5     116. #> # ℹ 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 664 × 3 #>   score  freq Grade #>   <int> <int> <chr> #> 1    86     8 M1    #> 2    87    11 M1    #> 3    88     4 M1    #> 4    89     3 M1    #> 5    90     3 M1    #> # ℹ 659 more rows  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                grade = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

title: specifying-manual-cut-scores
code: library(EmStanS) set.seed(100) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300)    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2… #> # ℹ 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 × 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # ℹ 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 × 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level1 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level2 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level3 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level2 #> # ℹ 2,245 more rows #>  #> $item_data #> # A tibble: 90 × 7 #>   GCA   Domain Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50 #>   <chr> <chr>  <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl> #> 1 M1    Alg    M1_1               1     1                   1     111. #> 2 M1    Alg    M1_2               1     1                   2     134. #> 3 M1    Prob   M1_3               1     1                   3     134. #> 4 M1    Alg    M1_4               1     1                   4     141. #> 5 M1    Geom   M1_5               1     1                   5     152. #> # ℹ 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 615 × 3 #>   score  freq Grade #>   <int> <int> <chr> #> 1   100     6 M1    #> 2   101     5 M1    #> 3   102     2 M1    #> 4   103     6 M1    #> 5   104     8 M1    #> # ℹ 610 more rows  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                grade = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

title: Embedded Standard Setting
code: library(EmStanS) # data <- read.csv(filePath) fake_data <- genFakeDataSet(ngca = 3,                 cor_val = 0.2,                n = 30,                nlevel = 3,                ntable = 5,                npanelist = 5,                sdinp = 1,                ecinp = 0,                100,300) #> New names: #> New names: #> • `score` -> `score...1` #> • `freq` -> `freq...2` #> • `Grade` -> `Grade...3` #> • `score` -> `score...4` #> • `freq` -> `freq...5` #> • `Grade` -> `Grade...6`    fake_data #> $setup #> # A tibble: 3 × 7 #>   GCA   `Content Area` `Grade Group` `Number of Tables per Room` `Level Options` #>   <chr> <chr>          <chr>         <chr>                       <chr>           #> 1 M1    Math           1             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 2 M2    Math           2             5                           Level1, Level2… #> 3 M3    Math           3             5                           Level1, Level2… #> # ℹ 2 more variables: SD <dbl>, EC <dbl> #>  #> $panelist #> # A tibble: 75 × 3 #>   GCA   Panelist Table #>   <chr> <chr>    <chr> #> 1 M1    M1.T1.1  T1    #> 2 M1    M1.T1.2  T1    #> 3 M1    M1.T1.3  T1    #> 4 M1    M1.T1.4  T1    #> 5 M1    M1.T1.5  T1    #> # ℹ 70 more rows #>  #> $rating #> # A tibble: 2,250 × 8 #>   GCA   Subject Grade Round Table Panelist Item_ID ALD    #>   <chr> <chr>   <chr> <dbl> <chr> <chr>    <chr>   <chr>  #> 1 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_1    Level1 #> 2 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_2    Level2 #> 3 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_3    Level1 #> 4 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_4    Level2 #> 5 M1    Math    1         1 T1    M1.T1.1  M1_5    Level1 #> # ℹ 2,245 more rows #>  #> $item_data #> # A tibble: 90 × 6 #>   GCA   Item_ID Total_points Point Order_of_Difficulty Loc_RP50 #>   <chr> <chr>          <dbl> <dbl>               <int>    <dbl> #> 1 M1    M1_1               1     1                   1     101. #> 2 M1    M1_2               1     1                   2     116. #> 3 M1    M1_3               1     1                   3     116. #> 4 M1    M1_4               1     1                   4     119. #> 5 M1    M1_5               1     1                   5     126. #> # ℹ 85 more rows #>  #> $examineedata_data #> # A tibble: 229 × 9 #>   score...1 freq...2 Grade...3 score...4 freq...5 Grade...6 score...7 freq...8 #>       <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> <chr>         <int>    <int> #> 1        87        5 M1               98        7 M2              127        5 #> 2        88        8 M1               99        9 M2              128        5 #> 3        89        3 M1              100        3 M2              129        6 #> 4        90        4 M1              101        4 M2              130        7 #> 5        91        6 M1              102        7 M2              131        8 #> # ℹ 224 more rows #> # ℹ 1 more variable: Grade...9 <chr>  # Or you can use your own data structured by the following table. res <- emstans(data = fake_data,                tests = c(1,2),                targets = "ALD",                WESS = T,                gamest = F,                median = "modal",                loc = "Loc_RP50",                domain = "GCA")

Embedded Standard Setting — EmStanS-package
text: Embedded Standard Setting (ESS) approach establish performance standards augments embedded within existing principled assessment design (PAD) practices. ESS, achievement level descriptor (ALD) writing alignment test items specified achievement levels viewed fundamental standard-setting activities cut scores estimated optimizing relationship item-ALD alignments empirically. ESS, package provides cut score estimation algorithm (referred ESS-Count ESS-Weight).

title: Embedded Standard Setting — EmStanS-package
text: Maintainer: Sooyong Lee sooyongl09@utexas.edu Authors: Sooyong Lee sooyongl09@utexas.edu Daniel Lewis [copyright holder] Seung Choi

what: Generate a Fake Data Set for Embedded Standard Setting — genFakeDataSet
title: Generate a Fake Data Set for Embedded Standard Setting — genFakeDataSet
text: `genFakeDataSet()` generates simulated data set follows input structure required `emstans()`. generated data can used test functions EmStanS package, demonstrate expected data format, run examples without using operational data.

title: Generate a Fake Data Set for Embedded Standard Setting — genFakeDataSet
code: genFakeDataSet(   ngca,   ntable,   npanelist,   cor_val,   nlevel,   sdinp = 1,   ecinp = 0,   n,   ... )

title: Generate a Fake Data Set for Embedded Standard Setting — genFakeDataSet
text: ngca numeric value indicating number grade clusters GCAs generate. ntable numeric value indicating number tables generate within GCA. npanelist numeric value indicating number panelists generate within table. cor_val numeric value indicating intended correlation item locations aligned level descriptions. nlevel numeric value indicating number performance levels aligned level-description categories. sdinp numeric value used adjust standard deviation values used Weighted Embedded Standard Setting. Default `1`. ecinp numeric value used control essentially consistent items simulated item ratings. Default `0`. n numeric value indicating number items generate within GCA. ... Additional numeric arguments passed `runif()`, typically minimum maximum score values used generate item locations. example, `100, 300` generates item locations 100 300.

title: Generate a Fake Data Set for Embedded Standard Setting — genFakeDataSet
text: list simulated data frames formatted use `emstans()`.   returned list contains test setup information, panelist information,   item ratings, item metadata, student data.

title: Generate a Fake Data Set for Embedded Standard Setting — genFakeDataSet
text: function mainly intended package testing, examples, demonstrations. returned object mimics data structure expected `emstans()`, allowing users inspect required format preparing data.

what: Get predictions from a GAM model. — predict_gam
title: Get predictions from a GAM model. — predict_gam
text: `r lifecycle::badge("superseded")` function superseded package tidymv. Please, use tidygam package instead. returns tibble predictions terms [gam][mgcv::gam] [bam][mgcv::bam] model. simply want return tibble predicted values response/outcome variable based terms (minus excluded smooth terms), set `type = "link"` (default). Note `type = "link"`, parametric terms excluded prediction, due limitations `mgcv`. want return tibble predicted values response/outcome variable term model separately, set `type = "terms"`. type can helpful want flexibility plotting.

title: Get predictions from a GAM model. — predict_gam
code: predict_gam(   model,   exclude_terms = NULL,   length_out = 50,   values = NULL,   type = "link" )

title: Get predictions from a GAM model. — predict_gam
text: model `gam` `bam` model object. exclude_terms Terms excluded prediction. Term names given appear model summary (example, `"s(x0,x1)"`). length_out integer indicating many values along numeric predictors use predicting outcome term (default `50`). values User supplied values specific terms named list. value `NULL`, first value term selected (useful excluding terms). type Either `"link"` `"terms"`. See Details .

title: Get predictions from a GAM model. — predict_gam
Get predictions from a GAM model. — predict_gam
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ library(mgcv) set.seed(10) data <- gamSim(4) model <- gam(y ~ fac + s(x2) + s(x2, by = fac) + s(x0), data = data)  # get predictions p <- predict_gam(model)  # get predictions excluding x0 (the coefficient of x0 is set to 0); # setting the value for the excluded term to NULL with the argument 'values' # reduces computation time p_2 <- predict_gam(model, exclude_terms = "s(x0)", values = list(x0 = NULL))  # get predictions with chosen values of x0  p_3 <- predict_gam(model, values = list(x0 = c(0.250599, 0.503313, 0.756028))) } # }
